nysed-ela-2025-g3-mc-q1.alt: Question 1. In paragraph 7, what does Sylvie mean when she says, “Sounds more stellar, doesn’t it?” Choices: A: She wants people to think the event will be amazing. B: She wants people to be sure art is part of the event. C: She wants people to bring their own paint to the event. D: She wants people to know the event will help animals.

nysed-ela-2025-g3-mc-q2.alt: Question 2. What do the details in paragraphs 2 and 9 show about Sylvie? Choices: A: She likes playing on the swings. B: She cares about animals. C: She knows a lot about the town. D: She is a talented artist.

nysed-ela-2025-g3-mc-q3.alt: Question 3. Based on paragraph 14, why does Sammy make a confused face in paragraph 15? Choices: A: He is surprised that Sylvie thinks it is getting late. B: He is disappointed that Sylvie was ready before him. C: He is worried his parents did not pack everything. D: He is not sure why they need a booth for the dog.

nysed-ela-2025-g3-mc-q4.alt: Question 4. “Preparation is the key to success” is a common saying that means when you plan for an event, it is more likely to go well. Which paragraph best shows how this idea is demonstrated in the story? Choices: A: paragraph 1 B: paragraph 3 C: paragraph 11 D: paragraph 15

nysed-ela-2025-g3-mc-q5.alt: Question 5. Which detail best expresses a central idea of the story? Choices: A: “‘Hmm.’ Sammy tilted his head. ‘I have a few ideas too.’” (paragraph 3) B: “Then we can sell the paintings. We can donate the money to the Sea View Animal Shelter.” (paragraph 9) C: “Sylvie looked around. A balloon arch spelled ‘Happy Make a Difference Day’ from streetlight to streetlight.” (paragraph 19) D: “Sylvie nodded. She squeezed in a few warm-up stretches and reached for her megaphone.” (paragraph 26)

nysed-ela-2025-g3-mc-q6.alt: Question 6. By the end of the story, how does Sylvie most likely feel about the Paint Out? Choices: A: She is relieved that the planning and work are finished. B: She is upset because she did most of the work. C: She is concerned that they did not raise enough money. D: She is proud that the event was a huge success.

nysed-ela-2025-g3-mc-q19.alt: Question 19. How does the graphic support the information in paragraph 2? Choices: A: by showing what raccoons look like B: by explaining that raccoons have good hearing C: by explaining that raccoons can see at night D: by showing where some raccoons live

nysed-ela-2025-g3-mc-q20.alt: Question 20. Which sentence best explains why raccoons mostly come out at night? Choices: A: Raccoons find their food when it is dark. B: Raccoons spend most of their time alone. C: Raccoons hide during the day to keep safe. D: Raccoons have black masks and tail rings.

nysed-ela-2025-g3-mc-q21.alt: Question 21. In which section would the reader find information about what raccoons do during the winter? Choices: A: “Flexible Fingers” B: “Eating Almost Anything” C: “Night and Day” D: “Many Different Dens”

nysed-ela-2025-g3-mc-q22.alt: Question 22. Animals learn to do things a certain way because of their habitat. Which sentence from the passage best connects to this idea? Choices: A: “Raccoon paws have five fingers, just like a person’s hands do.” (paragraph 2) B: “Exactly what a raccoon eats depends on where it lives.” (paragraph 4) C: “They use the many nerves on the bottoms of their paws to feel their food.” (paragraph 5) D: “In this home range, they have many hiding places that they use as dens.” (paragraph 8)

nysed-ela-2025-g3-mc-q23.alt: Question 23. Which detail from the passage best shows what the author thinks about raccoons? Choices: A: “Raccoons are very smart and quick.” (paragraph 3) B: “Raccoons living near people also eat pet food...” (paragraph 4) C: “Raccoons often dip their food in water...” (paragraph 5) D: “Raccoons can sometimes spread diseases to people.” (paragraph 11)

nysed-ela-2025-g3-mc-q26.alt: Question 26. Why is the information in paragraph 3 important to the story? Choices: A: It shows the cause of a problem in the story. B: It gives details about the setting. C: It gives a hint about how the story will end. D: It introduces a new character.

nysed-ela-2025-g3-mc-q27.alt: Question 27. Which meaning of the word “spread” is used in paragraph 5? Choices: A: scattered around B: grew slowly C: became known D: shared freely

nysed-ela-2025-g3-mc-q28.alt: Question 28. Which detail from the story best tells why Marta begins crying? Choices: A: “It went to Marta’s stomach...” (paragraph 2) B: “They were in such a hurry to run to the cookhouse that they knocked over Marta’s table...” (paragraph 3) C: “But the dogs, too, were chasing. Leaping into the air, they barked to each other. A game!” (paragraph 7) D: “Ruined homework! Broken glasses!” (paragraph 10)

nysed-ela-2025-g3-mc-q29.alt: Question 29. Which detail from the story best tells what Grandmother believes? Choices: A: “Grandmother’s big moon tortillas were the best in the world!” (paragraph 1) B: “When we have a problem we must choose what we will be.” (paragraph 16) C: “When the eagle is high up, it sees how small the earth is.” (paragraph 19) D: “Grandmother wound some tape around the arm of the glasses.” (paragraph 20)

nysed-ela-2025-g3-mc-q30.alt: Question 30. What does Marta most likely think about Grandmother’s healing song? Choices: A: It reminds her of a story. B: It helps her make a choice. C: It makes her want to dance. D: It teaches her about animals.

nysed-ela-2025-g3-mc-q31.alt: Question 31. Which detail would be most important to include in a summary of the story? Choices: A: Marta’s stomach makes loud noises. B: The tortillas are in the cookhouse. C: The tortillas bubble and turn brown. D: Marta decides to be like an eagle.
